explanation: This is the original command line used for testing the Sigma rule. It is not a bypass but a valid test case.
Substitution of long options to short options where applicable (e.g., --processId to -processId) is a safe mutation technique.
Substitution of long options to short options where applicable (e.g., --file to -file) is a safe mutation technique.
Use of equals (=) instead of space for argument assignment is a safe mutation technique.
Reordering of arguments where the program accepts positional arguments is a safe mutation technique.
Appending a harmless '.txt' at the end of the command line may bypass the 'CommandLine|contains: .txt' detection logic while still being valid for testing.
Appending '--' to indicate end of options is a safe mutation technique and may bypass some simplistic string-based detection logic.
Appending a backslash at the end of the command line is a safe mutation technique and may bypass some simplistic string-based detection logic.
Appending quotes at the end of the command line is a safe mutation technique and may bypass some simplistic string-based detection logic.
